message:
fix: use terminal default background to prevent ANSI color mismap (#101), add auto release notes (#99)

Issue #101: The markdown renderer hardcoded Dracula background (#282a36) for
dark mode, which gets downconverted to ANSI color index 2 (green) on
terminals with remapped ANSI slots (e.g., Solarized Dark in macOS
Terminal.app). Fix by using nil (terminal default) background for both
light and dark modes, matching how light mode already worked. Also
remove explicit Background(ColorBgDark) from footer filler/hint
elements that caused low-contrast blocks on non-standard themes.

Issue #99: Add release-notes.yml workflow that triggers on release
creation and auto-generates notes via GitHub API when the body is
empty or minimal (<10 chars).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,21 @@
+name: Auto Release Notes
+
+on:
+  release:
+    types: [created]
+
+jobs:
+  notes:
+    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
+    permissions:
+      contents: write
+    steps:
+      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
+      - name: Generate release notes if body is empty
+        env:
+          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
+        run: |
+          BODY=$(gh release view "${{ github.event.release.tag_name }}" --json body -q .body)
+          if [ -z "$BODY" ] || [ ${#BODY} -lt 10 ]; then
+            gh release edit "${{ github.event.release.tag_name }}" --generate-notes
+          fi

@@ -175,15 +175,17 @@ func buildStyleFromTheme(theme Theme, isDark bool) ansi.StyleConfig {
 	mutedColor := extractHex(theme.Muted, isDark)
 	blockedColor := extractHex(theme.Blocked, isDark)
 
-	// Base document style - Dracula dark background or transparent for light mode
-	var docBgPtr *string
+	// Base document style - use terminal default background for both modes.
+	// Previously dark mode set an explicit Dracula background (#282a36), but
+	// terminals that remap ANSI color slots (e.g., Solarized Dark) could
+	// render it as vivid green (ANSI #2) when the hex value is downconverted
+	// to the 16-color palette. Using nil lets the terminal's own background
+	// show through, which is correct for every theme. (fixes #101)
+	var docBgPtr *string // nil = terminal default background
 	var docFg string
 	if isDark {
-		docBg := "#282a36"
-		docBgPtr = &docBg
 		docFg = "#f8f8f2"
 	} else {
-		docBgPtr = nil // No background for light mode (use terminal default)
 		docFg = "#000000"
 	}
